evaluations.name: Happy path — full planning workflow
evaluations.prompt: Plan a feature: add user notifications to a Next.js app with a PostgreSQL database. Users should receive in-app notifications for comments on their posts and be able to mark them as read.
expected_behaviors: Conducts discovery interview with concrete options and tradeoffs
Asks about real-time vs polling, notification preferences, and UI placement
Performs codebase analysis identifying relevant files and patterns
Produces a plan with specific file paths, targets, actions, and reasoning
Includes dependency ordering between changes
Lists concrete edge cases with mitigations
Writes testable validation criteria
Persists plan to a markdown file

evaluations.name: Light planning — scope check triggers short outline
evaluations.prompt: Plan a feature: rename the 'Settings' nav item to 'Preferences' across the app.
expected_behaviors: Recognizes this as light planning scope (single concern, clear requirements)
Does NOT conduct a full multi-phase planning workflow
Provides a brief outline: what files, what changes, why
Skips research phase entirely

evaluations.name: Stress-test round handling
evaluations.prompt: Plan a feature: add role-based access control (RBAC) to an existing Express API. The API currently has no auth beyond API keys. Roles needed: admin, editor, viewer. After planning, run the stress-test rounds.
expected_behaviors: Produces a complete plan before stress-testing
Launches stress-test subagents with ONLY the plan text, not conversation history
Presents subagent findings to the user after each round
Asks the user which findings to address before incorporating
Incorporates accepted feedback into the plan
Runs up to 3 rounds (stops early if no issues found)

evaluations.name: Edge case — user says skip research
evaluations.prompt: Plan a feature: add dark mode toggle to a React app. Skip the research phase, I already know what I want — just a CSS variables approach with a toggle button in the header.
expected_behaviors: Respects the user's request to skip research
Does NOT launch research sub-agents
Still conducts discovery interview for remaining ambiguities (persistence, system preference detection, transition animations)
Produces a plan that uses the user's specified approach (CSS variables)
Does not override user's stated preferences with research-based alternatives
